Flow:   MercaInvoice pays merchant directly OR calls settle() to hold funds here   Merchant calls withdraw() to pull funds to any address   Merchant can split across sub-wallets for params.name: Label for this wallet (e.g. "main", "savings", "tax").
params.withdrawTo: Address funds are sent to when withdrawing from this wallet.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

params.amount: Amount to withdraw. Use type(uint256).max for full balance.

deactivateSubWallet(uint256,address).notice: Deactivate a sub-wallet (must have zero balance first).

moveBalance(address,uint256,uint256,uint.notice: Move funds between sub-wallets (internal accounting only, no transfer).

settle(address,address,uint256,uint256).notice: Called by MercaInvoice to settle funds into a merchant's wallet.

updateSubWallet(uint256,address).notice: Update the withdrawal address of a sub-wallet.
